ab: wacogoso ki vaiyana?
ch: M:你今天有空嘛?
en: Are you available today?

ab: ai twatoma?
ch: W:要做什麼嗎?

ab: aiyowako ’olidraw mwswa.
ch: M:我想去拜訪你。
en: I would like to visit you.

ab: yakai ko atomananeli kaiyasa, lwigaigana.
ch: W:我今天有事不在家。改天吧。
en: I am out today. How about another day?

ab: wacogoso loza? naw mwako ’olidraw mwswa lo makatokodrali.
ch: M:那你明天有空嗎?我下午去拜訪你。
en: Then, are you available tomorrow? I’ll visit you tomorrow afternoon.

ab: ahoa miniya, kialalavalavako mwswa ikai naiyana.
ch: W:可以，我在家等你。
en: Sure, I will wait for you at home.

ab: na kaleketako roko’ing? asanga nawako athoro ki ’acase.
ch: M:請問我可以錄音嗎?我想做紀錄。
en: Would you mind if  I do the voice recording? I would like to keep a record.

ab: eene, ini kiyatoma.
ch: W:好啊!沒有問題。
en: Sure! No problem.

ab: kaleketako roko’ing sasing?
ch: M:可以錄影嗎?
en: Would you mind if I do the video recording?

ab: ini,malisabolako kadro mathariri, kwadra lriba miya kadro liethele, ini ki roko’ingi.
ch: W:我不好看，倉庫也沒整理，不要啦。
en: No, I am plain and the warehouse hasn’t been tidied up yet.

ab: lo miya iniya na sasingi katha.
ch: M:那我拍照就好。
en: Then I will just take photos.

ab: ini,lo sasingiya kadro mathariri.
ch: W:拍照也不要。
en: No photographing either.

ab: maelanenga so anialralrainginga kielaela ko moswa.
ch: M:謝謝你讓我訪問。
en: Thank you for accepting the interview.

ab: kadro kiyatoma, ai elaela so sa manema?
ch: W:沒問題，你想問什麼?
en: You are welcome. What would you like to know?

ab: sa yacengecenge so manema ka atomananeso?
ch: M:請問您年輕時是做什麼的?
en: What do you do when you are young?

ab: kodra tolavalavazaneli ka kengesase, wadanonongako kaiyasa.
ch: W:我以前是警察，但我現在退休了。
en: I was a police officer but I am retired now.

ab: yakai na atomatomana so kaiyasasa?
ch: M:那你現在有工作嗎?
en: Then, are you working at the moment?

ab: yakaiyako ki tatakesikesiya twatwatoma.
ch: W:我在學校上班。
en: I am working at school.

ab: miya yakaikai cekele ki lalakeso?
ch: M:請問您的孩子也住在部落嗎?
en: Do your kids live in the tribe too?

ab: ini,ko lalakeli yakaikai tokai.
ch: W:不，我的孩子在都市居住。
en: No, my kids are all living in the city.

ab: tangeangea so?
ch: M:那你自己一個人住嗎?
en: Then, do you live by yourself?

ab: wala’o ako taw’ong si azazame,miya ka wacekecekeleli.
ch: W:我還有養一隻狗和一隻鳥，他們也是我的家人。
en: I also keep a dog and a bird. They are my family.

ab: miya wala’o ako ngiyaw,yakai lo doboco to’a’ala kadro kase’elre.
ch: M:我也有養一隻貓，有動物陪伴不會無聊。
en: I have a cat too. I won’t get bored with the company of my pet.

ab: ai kielaelako moswa idra sana twalisiya ki alro’o.
ch: M:我想請問你關於打獵的禁忌。
en: I would like to ask you about the taboos of hunting.

ab: wathingalako ka bekela,lamani ai tobalrako moswa sanaka thingaleli.
ch: W:我知道的不多，但我能回答的都會回答你。
en: I don’t know much about that, but I will tell you everything I know.

ab: yakai sa manema ka lisi ki salro’a idra sarakaikaiya nomi alro’o?
ch: M:你們打獵的武器有什麼禁忌嗎?
en: Do you have any taboos about hunting weapons?

ab: kwadra salro’a nai ka sarakaikaiya alro’a kadroy aniswasola’e ki lrabaybay.
ch: W:我們打獵的武器不可以讓女生碰到。
en: Our hunting weapons are untouchable to women.

ab: kalalekete ko lrabaybay to’a’ala alro’o?
ch: M:那女人可以一起去打獵嗎?
en: Then, can women go hunting together?

ab: ini, ko lrabaybay kadroy mobolra.
ch: W:不行，女人也不可以打獵。
en: No, women cannot go hunting either.
